traces.gt_findings: Lung volumes are low. Retrocardiac opacity with silhouetting of the left hemidiaphragm and lateral border of the descending aorta is nonspecific and could reflect any of a combination of atelectasis, focal pneumonia or even a small effusion. Right infrahilar opacity with slight indistinctness of the right heart border could reflect infection in the appropriate clinical scenario. Apparent elevation of the right hemidiaphragm may be related to positioning and technique versus volume loss. There is cardiomegaly, but no CHF. Aortic calcifications are present. No pneumothorax.
traces.gt_impression: 1. Nonspecific retrocardiac and right middle lobe opacities could reflect atelectasis and/or infection in the appropriate clinical situation. 2. There is cardiomegaly without CHF.

steps.findings: Cardiomediastinal contours are within normal limits allowing for rotation. Lung volumes are low. Bibasilar atelectasis is present, worse on the right, but there are no definite pleural effusions. Left hemidiaphragm is mildly elevated. No visible pneumothorax.

Cardiomediastinal contours are within normal limits allowing for rotation. Lung volumes are low. Bibasilar atelectasis is present, worse on the right, but there are no definite pleural effusions. Left hemidiaphragm is mildly elevated. No visible pneumothorax.

The patient is status post recent esophagectomy procedure. Right chest tube is in place with a small right apical pneumothorax. Postoperative widening of right mediastinal contour is consistent with neoesophagus. Right hemidiaphragm is elevated, and there is atelectasis at the right base. Left lung is clear except for minor atelectasis in the left perihilar and basilar regions.

The patient is status post recent esophagectomy procedure. Right chest tube is in place with a small right apical pneumothorax. Postoperative widening of right mediastinal contour is consistent with neoesophagus. Right hemidiaphragm is elevated, and there is atelectasis at the right base. Left lung is clear except for minor atelectasis in the left perihilar and basilar regions.

Right chest tube remains in place, with persistent small right apical pneumothorax. Right juxtahilar and basilar lung opacities have slightly improved. Left lung is grossly clear except for minimal linear atelectasis at the left base. Moderate gastric distention is noted in the imaged portion of the upper abdomen.

Right chest tube remains in place, with persistent small right apical pneumothorax. Right juxtahilar and basilar lung opacities have slightly improved. Left lung is grossly clear except for minimal linear atelectasis at the left base. Moderate gastric distention is noted in the imaged portion of the upper abdomen.

Right chest tube remains in place, with persistent small right pleural effusion and no visible pneumothorax. Cardiomediastinal contours are stable in appearance. Worsening atelectasis at right lung base, and slight improvement in atelectasis at left lung base.

Right chest tube remains in place, with persistent small right pleural effusion and no visible pneumothorax. Cardiomediastinal contours are stable in appearance. Worsening atelectasis at right lung base, and slight improvement in atelectasis at left lung base.

Right chest tube remains in place, with persistent moderate right pleural effusion, but no visible pneumothorax. Patchy and linear atelectasis persists in the left retrocardiac region. Subcutaneous emphysema is again demonstrated in the right chest wall.
